source.title: Cortico-Striatal Activity Characterizes Human Safety Learning via Pavlovian Conditioned Inhibition

confidence_rationale: 7T human fMRI study dissociating conditioned inhibitor (actively predicts threat omission when paired with known threat signal) from standard safety signal (passively unpaired with threat). The dissociation is architecturally important: the conditioned inhibitor recruits dorsal striatum + anterior insula + dlPFC; the standard safety signal recruits vmPFC + hippocampus + posterior cingulate. The standard safety signal substrate (vmPFC/hippocampus) aligns with MECH-303's prefrontal-hippocampal claim. But whether MECH-303's safety-cue prediction store corresponds to the conditioned inhibitor or the standard safety signal pathway is unresolved -- if MECH-303 is about active prediction of suffering-absence (teaching-signal driven), it may be the striatal/conditioned-inhibitor route, not the vmPFC/hippocampal one.
failure_signatures: The conditioned inhibitor (which most closely approximates a teaching-signal-driven predictive safety store) recruits dorsal striatum and dlPFC -- not vmPFC/hippocampus. If MECH-303 is about the actively-learned 'this cue predicts safety' store, this result places the substrate in the wrong circuit.

The study used healthy human participants. The vmPFC/hippocampus finding for standard safety signals may reflect retrieval or expression of safety rather than the encoding/updating of the predictive store. These are confounded in the blocked fMRI design.
No single-unit resolution; cannot distinguish encoding from retrieval, or distinguish safety-prediction updating from safety-prediction retrieval expression.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Conditioned inhibition (actively predicting threat omission) engages dorsal striatal cortico-striatal circuitry; standard safety signals (CS-) engage vmPFC + hippocampus. These two forms of safety learning dissociate at the neural level in humans.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-303 posits a prefrontal-hippocampal substrate (IL/vmPFC-equivalent + vHipp-PL-equivalent). The vmPFC + hippocampus finding for standard safety signals directly supports this. However, if MECH-303's safety-cue prediction store is driven by the MECH-302 relief-completion event as a teaching signal -- making it analogous to a conditioned inhibitor -- then the dorsal striatum / cortico-striatal finding is the more relevant mapping, which would challenge the prefrontal-hippocampal claim.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The conditioned inhibitor vs. standard safety signal distinction is crucial and underspecified in MECH-303. MECH-303 posits a teaching signal (MECH-302 event), making the predictive safety store more similar to a conditioned inhibitor than a passively acquired standard safety signal. This paper suggests those two cases may have different neural substrates -- a distinction MECH-303 does not currently address.
mapping.source_context: 49 healthy human participants, ultra-high field 7T fMRI, Pavlovian conditioned inhibition paradigm, two types of safety signal contrasted within-subjects.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (7T fMRI, n=49, whole-brain corrected, peer-reviewed J Neurosci). Mapping fidelity is lower because the conditioned inhibitor vs standard safety signal distinction cuts across MECH-303's claim in a way the claim does not anticipate. The human fMRI setting means low rodent-to-human transfer risk, but high encoding-vs-retrieval confound risk.
